title: Deterministic cache key generation
description:
**As a** caching system, **I want** identical text+model+version inputs to always produce the same cache key, **so that** cached embeddings are reliably reused and invalidated when parameters change.

Cache key = `SHA-256(normalize(text) + "|" + model_id + "|" + preprocessing_version)`. Normalization collapses whitespace and lowercases. This story is pure logic with no I/O — it establishes the deterministic foundation the entire cache depends on.

Cache invalidation happens naturally: bumping `model_id` or `preprocessing_version` produces entirely new keys, so old cached values are simply never hit again.

## Key Files

- Create: `plexus/analysis/embedding_cache.py`
- Create: `plexus/analysis/embedding_cache_test.py`

## Dependencies

None.

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text:
Feature: Deterministic embedding cache key generation

  Scenario: Identical text produces identical cache keys
    Given text = "Hello world", model_id = "all-MiniLM-L6-v2", version = "v1"
    When cache_key() is called twice
    Then both calls return the same 64-character hex string (SHA-256)

  Scenario: Whitespace normalization produces stable keys
    Given text_a = "  Hello   world  
" and text_b = "hello world"
    And model_id = "all-MiniLM-L6-v2" and version = "v1"
    When cache_key() is computed for both
    Then the keys are identical

  Scenario: Different model_id produces different keys
    Given identical text and version but model_id "model-A" vs "model-B"
    When cache_key() is computed for both
    Then the keys differ

  Scenario: Different preprocessing version produces different keys
    Given identical text and model_id but version "v1" vs "v2"
    When cache_key() is computed for both
    Then the keys differ
